content: Curiosity-driven autonomous agents (Topic A) can embed a hybrid planning-learning loop that splits memory into two specialized sub-modules: one that handles incremental, learning-based updates to agent memory based on intrinsic reward signals from ICM/RND mechanisms, and another that performs deliberate, top-down planning to prune low-value novel exploration and prioritize high-uncertainty regions aligned with long-term agent goals. This architecture outperforms both pure curiosity-driven and pure planning-based agents in open-ended environments with sparse extrinsic rewards.
